D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Why Clouds Are White: Mie Scattering

grow a droplet from molecule to raindrop and watch blue scattering turn white

Scattering strength depends on the size parameter x = 2πr/λ. For tiny particles it scales as λ⁻⁴ (blue wins); for large droplets it flattens to nearly the same value at all colours.

Slide the droplet radius across six decades. The curve is scattering efficiency vs wavelength; the swatch is the resulting scattered colour — blue for molecules, white for cloud droplets.
